key_finding: A one percentage point decrease in pension discount rate assumptions is associated with a 50-80 basis point increase in real estate allocation targets and a 15-25 basis point compression in cap rates beyond what interest rate levels alone predict, suggesting pension liability management creates a demand channel that partially decouples cap rates from economic fundamentals.
novelty_rationale: The corpus studies institutional investor sentiment and capital flows but has not isolated pension liability-driven investment as a distinct mechanistic channel that creates systematic demand pressure on commercial real estate cap rates independent of return expectations.
nn_title: Real Estate in an ALM Framework: The Case of Fair Value Accounting
assigned_label: Real Estate Asset Valuation and Returns

research_question: Can transfer learning from satellite imagery trained on commercial real estate physical characteristics predict property income performance, and does this model capture information orthogonal to existing hedonic and financial variables?
method: Convolutional neural network trained on high-resolution aerial imagery to extract physical condition features, then used in a transfer learning framework to predict net operating income growth and vacancy changes, with orthogonality tests against CoStar quality ratings and financial predictors using out-of-sample cross-validation.
data: Nearmap and Google Earth Engine 10cm-resolution aerial imagery for approximately 200,000 commercial properties in 20 U.S. metros, matched to CoStar financial performance data including NOI, vacancy, and capital expenditure records from 2015-2023.
key_finding: Image-derived physical condition scores explain an additional 5-12% of NOI growth variance beyond CoStar quality ratings and financial controls, with the largest incremental predictability for older vintage properties where physical heterogeneity is greatest and where traditional data is least reliable.
novelty_rationale: The corpus applies machine learning to price prediction using structured data and tests ML classifiers for building vintage, but has not used deep learning on satellite imagery as an independent signal for commercial real estate income performance orthogonal to existing financial data.
nn_title: Information value of property description: A Machine learning approach
assigned_label: Hedonic pricing and residential location choice

research_question: How does the spatial concentration of remote workers within suburbs affect local retail vacancy rates, restaurant survival, and the commercial real estate ecosystem of previously dormant suburban business districts?
method: Exploiting variation in the share of remote-compatible occupations in suburban census tracts pre-pandemic as a shift-share instrument, estimating causal effects on retail lease vacancy, restaurant opening and closing rates, and commercial asking rents using a difference-in-differences framework that compares high versus low remote-work-share suburban submarkets.
data: CoStar retail lease vacancy and asking rent data at the submarket level merged with Yelp business opening/closing records, Census LODES commuting data, and BLS occupational employment statistics for suburban ZIP codes across 50 U.S. metros from 2018-2024.
key_finding: A 10 percentage point higher pre-pandemic remote work share in a suburban submarket is associated with a 3-6 percentage point reduction in retail vacancy and a 8-15% increase in restaurant openings post-2020, suggesting remote work is reshaping the economic geography of suburban commercial real estate more than office market data alone reveals.
novelty_rationale: The corpus studies COVID effects on office and residential markets but has not examined the second-order effect of remote worker spatial reallocation on suburban retail commercial real estate ecosystems as a distinct and economically significant consequence of changed commuting patterns.
nn_title: The remote work revolution: Impact on real estate values and the urban environme
assigned_label: Spatial Economics and Urban Land Values

research_question: Does the mandatory inclusionary zoning requirement to include affordable units in market-rate developments create a capitalization wedge that is shared asymmetrically between landowners, developers, and market-rate buyers depending on local supply elasticity?
method: Structural model of developer land acquisition and pricing behavior estimated using a regression discontinuity at inclusionary zoning density thresholds, decomposing the IZ cost into land price reductions absorbed by sellers, market-rate price increases passed to buyers, and developer profit compression, with heterogeneity by housing supply elasticity.
data: Parcel-level land transaction records and residential permit applications from 30 jurisdictions with IZ requirements, merged with CoreLogic sale price data, and housing supply elasticity estimates, covering 2000-2023.
key_finding: Inclusionary zoning costs are split approximately 40% to landowners, 35% to market-rate buyers, and 25% to developer margins in supply-constrained markets, but the incidence shifts substantially toward buyers and developers in more elastic markets where land price depression is harder to achieve, revealing IZ as a regressive tax in constrained cities.
novelty_rationale: The corpus studies zoning restrictions and affordable housing policy broadly but has not structurally estimated the full incidence distribution of inclusionary zoning costs across landowners, developers, and market-rate buyers as a function of local supply conditions.
nn_title: Urban land value functions with endogenous zoning
assigned_label: Land use regulation and property values

research_question: How does the geographic co-location of university research parks with residential neighborhoods affect the hedonic price gradient of housing, and does proximity to research parks accelerate local human capital accumulation and startup density in ways that further compound capitalization?
method: Synthetic control and difference-in-differences analysis exploiting the staggered establishment and expansion of university research parks, estimating house price gradients around parks before and after establishment and testing for complementary effects on startup density and educational attainment over a 10-20 year horizon.
data: Association of University Research Parks membership data with establishment dates merged with CoreLogic housing transactions, Census tract educational attainment changes, Crunchbase startup founding records by location, and BLS employment data from 1990-2023.
key_finding: University research park establishment increases residential prices within a half-mile by 6-12% within five years, with an additional 3-5% appreciation over the subsequent decade as startup density and skilled labor inflows compound, generating total capitalization effects comparable to transit infrastructure investments.
novelty_rationale: The corpus studies transit, school quality, and airport effects on real estate but has not examined university research park establishment as a housing market amenity that creates dynamic human capital and economic development spillovers that compound over time through agglomeration.
nn_title: Housing Regulation, Externalities and Residential Property Prices
assigned_label: Hedonic pricing and residential location choice

research_question: Does the staggered introduction of rental registry and landlord licensing programs reduce housing code violations, improve tenant health outcomes, and affect rent levels and rental supply in ways that reveal the welfare trade-offs of tenant protection regulation?
method: Staggered difference-in-differences exploiting the variation in rental registry adoption across U.S. cities, estimating effects on housing code violation rates, emergency department visits for housing-related illness, rental prices, and the exit of small landlords from the market, with heterogeneity by enforcement intensity.
data: City housing code violation databases, hospital discharge records from HCUP for housing-related illness diagnoses, CoStar and Zillow rental listing data, property ownership records from county assessors, and rental registry program enactment dates for 150 U.S. cities from 2000-2023.
key_finding: Rental registry adoption reduces housing code violations by 15-25% and decreases emergency department visits for mold and pest-related illness by 8-12%, but increases rents by 2-4% and accelerates small-landlord exit by 10-15%, revealing a welfare trade-off between housing quality and rental affordability.
novelty_rationale: The corpus studies rent control and tenant protection extensively but has not evaluated rental registry and landlord licensing as a distinct regulatory intervention with a specific enforcement mechanism linking property inspection to health outcomes and supply-side landlord behavior.
nn_title: Information Asymmetry, Regulations and Equilibrium Outcomes: Theory and Evidence
assigned_label: Rental Markets, Policy Effects, and Housing Supply

research_question: How do corporate relocation decisions, driven by tax incentive packages from destination states, affect origin city commercial real estate values, retail ecosystems, and residential prices through the simultaneous departure of headquarter employment and high-earning households?
method: Event study using major corporate headquarters relocations (Amazon, Oracle, HP, Tesla) as case studies within a synthetic control framework, estimating divergent price trajectories in origin versus destination metros for both commercial and residential property with heterogeneity by worker income level and housing market elasticity.
data: SEC filings and press releases identifying 200+ corporate headquarters relocations from 1995-2023, merged with CoStar commercial real estate price indices, Zillow residential price indices, IRS migration data for high-income households, and BLS county employment records.
key_finding: Major headquarters departures decrease origin-city Class A office rents by 8-15% within three years and residential prices in nearby neighborhoods by 4-8%, with effects twice as large in supply-constrained cities, while destination metros experience symmetric gains with a 12-24 month lag driven by residential rather than commercial real estate first.
novelty_rationale: The corpus studies Amazon HQ2 price effects in a single event but has not systematically analyzed the two-sided market consequences of corporate relocations for both origin and destination real estate markets across a large sample of relocation events.
nn_title: Amazon is coming to town: Sequential information revelation in the housing marke
assigned_label: Local tax policy and fiscal interdependence

research_question: Does homeowner association (HOA) density and fee structure affect housing price dynamics, liquidity, and household sorting in ways that create a segmented housing market with distinct price cycles from non-HOA submarkets?
method: Repeat-sales price index construction separately for HOA and non-HOA properties within the same census tract, combined with a structural search model estimating buyer willingness to pay for HOA membership as a function of fee level, amenity bundle, and neighborhood composition, exploiting state-level HOA formation law changes as instruments.
data: CoreLogic property records with HOA indicator flags and fee data merged with deed transaction records for 30 million residential sales from 1995-2023, supplemented by HOA financial filings from states requiring disclosure and state HOA formation law timelines.
key_finding: HOA properties exhibit 20-30% lower price volatility than matched non-HOA properties in the same tract, trade at a 5-8% premium on average, but show significantly slower recovery from market downturns because HOA fee delinquency during recessions triggers special assessments that depress values, revealing a stabilization-recovery asymmetry.
novelty_rationale: Despite HOAs governing approximately 30% of U.S. housing, the corpus has essentially no papers examining HOA fee capitalization, price dynamics, or the governance mechanisms by which HOAs affect housing market outcomes and household sorting.
nn_title: –Nonrecourse Mortgage and Housing Price Boom, Bust, and Rebound
assigned_label: Hedonic pricing and residential location choice

research_question: How does the entry of iBuyers into local residential markets change the information environment by revealing previously latent price signals, and does iBuyer activity reduce or amplify the serial correlation in house price returns documented in the corpus?
method: Vector autoregression analysis of house price autocorrelation before and after iBuyer market entry, using the staggered geographic rollout of Opendoor, Offerpad, and Zillow Offers as a natural experiment, with a structural model of price discovery that distinguishes iBuyer transaction informativeness from liquidity provision effects.
data: Zillow transaction data identifying iBuyer purchases and resales by corporate entity matching, merged with FHFA house price index data at the ZIP code level and MLS transaction-level data capturing time-on-market and list-to-sale ratios for 2014-2023 in 30 metros.
key_finding: iBuyer market entry reduces ZIP-code-level house price autocorrelation by 20-35%, consistent with faster price discovery, but this efficiency improvement concentrates in the middle of the price distribution and actually increases price volatility at the tails where iBuyer algorithms are least calibrated.
novelty_rationale: The corpus discusses iBuyers' market impact on prices and time-on-market but has not examined the information economics of iBuyer activity—specifically whether algorithmic buying accelerates price discovery and reduces the serial correlation that is central to the corpus's housing return literature.
nn_title: The burgeoning role of iBuyers in the housing market
assigned_label: Real estate market intermediation and mechanisms

research_question: Does the rollout of gigabit broadband infrastructure causally increase residential property values, and does this effect vary by pre-existing remote-work intensity of local labor markets?
method: Difference-in-differences exploiting staggered municipal fiber rollout schedules as quasi-random variation in broadband availability, interacted with pre-pandemic remote-work share by occupation.
data: FCC Form 477 broadband availability data merged with CoreLogic transaction-level house price data and BLS Occupational Employment Statistics across U.S. ZIP codes from 2015–2023.
key_finding: Gigabit availability is likely to generate a 2–6% price premium concentrated in markets with high pre-existing remote-work potential, with diminishing returns where broadband was already adequate.
novelty_rationale: Prior hedonic studies measure broadband speed generically; this paper isolates fiber-to-the-premises shocks and shows that labor-market composition mediates the infrastructure-to-price channel in a post-COVID world.
nn_title: The remote work revolution: Impact on real estate values and the urban environme
assigned_label: Land use regulation and property values

research_question: How do maternal mortality rates and obstetric care deserts affect residential sorting, homeownership decisions, and house prices for households with young children?
method: Instrumental variables estimation using the staggered closure of rural obstetric units as an exogenous shock to local healthcare access, linked to household-level migration and tenure choice outcomes.
data: American Hospital Association annual surveys on hospital obstetric unit closures linked to HMDA loan originations, ACS household data, and Zillow ZIP-code price indices from 2000–2022.
key_finding: Obstetric unit closures likely reduce homeownership transitions by young-family households and depress home prices within affected commuting zones by 3–8%, with larger effects in rural areas lacking alternative facilities.
novelty_rationale: The capitalization of reproductive healthcare access into housing decisions is entirely unexplored in the literature, representing a novel amenity channel with clear policy relevance for rural housing markets.
nn_title: Housing Tenure Transitions of Older Households: What is the Role of Child Proxim
assigned_label: Residential mobility and migration determinants

research_question: Do commercial real estate appraisers exhibit systematic anchoring to prior appraisals during periods of rapidly rising interest rates that compress cap rates relative to fundamentals?
method: Regression discontinuity and panel analysis comparing appraised values to concurrent transaction prices during Fed tightening cycles, testing whether appraisal revision lags are asymmetric between rising and falling rate environments.
data: NCREIF property-level appraisal and transaction records for office, industrial, and multifamily assets from 1990–2023, matched to FRED Treasury yield series.
key_finding: Appraisers likely exhibit stronger anchoring during rate-rising cycles, with appraised values lagging transaction prices by 8–15% during the first four quarters of tightening, creating temporary valuation gaps that distort CMBS underwriting.
novelty_rationale: Existing appraisal smoothing literature focuses on declining markets; this paper identifies an asymmetric channel specific to monetary tightening that has material consequences for CMBS collateral quality assessments.
nn_title: Appraisal overvaluation: Evidence of price adjustment bias in sales Comparisons
assigned_label: Housing Market Efficiency and Pricing Anomalies

research_question: How does the introduction of automated eviction filing systems by corporate landlords affect eviction rates, tenant mobility, and neighborhood composition in multifamily rental markets?
method: Difference-in-differences exploiting staggered county-level adoption of electronic court filing systems for evictions, comparing outcomes in buildings owned by corporate versus non-corporate landlords.
data: Princeton Eviction Lab case-level eviction records merged with CoStar building ownership data and ACS neighborhood demographic data across 30 U.S. states from 2010–2022.
key_finding: Automated eviction filing access likely increases eviction filing rates by 15–25% among corporate landlords relative to small landlords and accelerates neighborhood demographic turnover in low-income census tracts.
novelty_rationale: The literature on corporate landlord behavior focuses on rents and eviction levels but does not isolate how transaction-cost reductions from legal technology asymmetrically empower large institutional landlords.
nn_title: The rise of corporate landlords: An examination of behavioral differences in the
assigned_label: Rental Markets, Policy Effects, and Housing Supply

research_question: Does exposure to wildfire smoke, distinct from direct fire damage, systematically reduce commercial real estate values and office lease renewal rates in Western U.S. markets?
method: Panel fixed-effects regression using satellite-derived daily PM2.5 smoke exposure at the building level as the treatment variable, with property-level fixed effects and staggered smoke events as quasi-experiments.
data: NOAA Hazard Mapping System wildfire smoke polygons linked to CoStar office building transactions and lease renewals across California, Oregon, and Washington from 2010–2023.
key_finding: Cumulative annual smoke days above 50 µg/m³ are expected to reduce office rents by 3–7% and materially increase vacancy rates, with larger effects for buildings lacking advanced HVAC filtration.
novelty_rationale: Existing climate-real estate literature focuses on flood and hurricane risk; smoke as a chronic air quality hazard distinct from fire proximity represents an underexplored mechanism with growing relevance.
nn_title: Commercial real estate and air pollution
assigned_label: Environmental hazards and property valuation

research_question: How does the staggered legalization of accessory dwelling unit construction across California municipalities affect the supply elasticity of multifamily rental housing and the spatial distribution of rent burdens?
method: Synthetic difference-in-differences exploiting variation in the timing and stringency of ADU ordinance passage across California cities, comparing rental supply and rent growth trajectories to matched control cities.
data: California HCD ADU permit records, CoStar multifamily rent data, and ACS income-to-rent burden statistics at the Census place level from 2015–2023.
key_finding: ADU legalization likely increases rental supply by 4–9% in high-demand jurisdictions over five years and reduces rent growth by 2–4 percentage points relative to controls, with smaller effects in cities where parking or setback requirements remain binding.
novelty_rationale: While ADU value effects have been studied, the supply elasticity and rent-burden consequences of ADU deregulation at scale using a quasi-experimental research design remains absent from the literature.
nn_title: The Effects of Rent Control Expansion on Tenants, Landlords, and Inequality: Evi
assigned_label: Rental Markets, Policy Effects, and Housing Supply

research_question: Can the term structure of CMBS credit spreads predict commercial real estate price declines at the metropolitan level with greater lead time than traditional cap rate or NOI-based indicators?
method: Predictive regression and real-time out-of-sample forecasting comparing CMBS spread curves (by loan vintage and remaining term) against standard fundamental forecasting models for MSA-level transaction price indices.
data: Trepp CMBS loan-level spread data linked to NCREIF and RCA transaction-based price indices across 30 U.S. MSAs from 2000–2023.
key_finding: The slope of the CMBS term-spread curve for a given MSA likely predicts price declines 2–4 quarters ahead with greater accuracy than cap rate or vacancy-based models, providing an early warning signal for macroprudential policy.
novelty_rationale: While CMBS default prediction is well-studied, using the cross-sectional term structure of secondary market spreads as a real-time leading indicator for private market price dynamics is unexplored.
nn_title: Pricing Credit Risk for Mortgages: Credit Risk Spreads and Heterogeneity across
assigned_label: Housing market dynamics and price determinants

research_question: Does the concentration of private equity-backed homebuilders in local markets distort land acquisition pricing and new construction supply relative to markets dominated by public homebuilders?
method: Matched market comparison using Herfindahl-Hirschman indices of private equity versus publicly traded builder market share, with instrumental variables based on PE dry powder cycles to identify supply distortions.
data: Census Bureau building permit data, BLS construction employment records, Dodge Data homebuilder market share statistics, and Preqin PE real estate fund data across U.S. MSAs from 2010–2023.
key_finding: Markets with high PE-backed builder concentration likely exhibit 10–20% higher land acquisition prices and more procyclical supply responses, exacerbating housing cycle volatility relative to markets dominated by public builders.
novelty_rationale: The homebuilding literature distinguishes public from private builders but has not examined how the organizational form and incentive structure of PE-backed developers specifically distorts land markets and supply timing.
nn_title: Arrested Development: Theory and Evidence of Supply‐Side Speculation in the Hous
assigned_label: Housing market dynamics and price determinants

research_question: How does heterogeneity in municipal pension funding shortfalls affect local property tax capitalization and homeowner forward-looking house price expectations?
method: Regression discontinuity and panel instrumental variables using state-mandated pension contribution schedules and actuarial assumption changes as exogenous shocks to expected future property tax burdens.
data: Public Plans Database pension funding ratios linked to CoreLogic house prices, Zillow home value expectations surveys, and county property tax levy data across 200 U.S. municipalities from 2005–2022.
key_finding: A 10 percentage point deterioration in pension funding ratio is expected to reduce house prices by 1.5–4% in fiscally constrained municipalities where property tax increases are the marginal revenue source, with larger effects near school district boundaries.
novelty_rationale: The fiscal capitalization literature focuses on current tax rates; this paper identifies a forward-looking channel where households capitalize expected future tax increases from pension liabilities into current prices.
nn_title: Property values and pension underfunding in the local public sector
assigned_label: Local tax policy and fiscal interdependence

research_question: Do REITs that adopt real-time asset-level IoT sensor networks for tenant monitoring and building operations achieve measurably superior operating efficiency and lower cost of capital relative to non-adopters?
method: Difference-in-differences using staggered IoT system installation announcements as treatment events, comparing NOI margins, G&A expense ratios, and bid-ask spreads before and after adoption controlling for property type fixed effects.
data: REIT 10-K disclosures on smart building investments, NCREIF operating income data, Bloomberg REIT liquidity metrics, and commercial real estate technology vendor deployment records from 2015–2023.
key_finding: IoT adoption is likely to reduce REIT property operating costs by 5–10% within three years and compress bid-ask spreads by 8–15 basis points as information asymmetry between managers and investors declines.
novelty_rationale: Technology adoption effects on REIT performance remain unstudied; this paper introduces PropTech investment as a firm-level value-creation mechanism distinct from asset-selection or geographic timing ability.
nn_title: The Role of the Underlying Real Asset Market in REIT IPOs
assigned_label: Real Estate Asset Valuation and Returns

research_question: How does the spatial clustering of mortgage servicer headquarters affect geographic variation in loss mitigation outcomes for borrowers in default, and does servicer proximity create information advantages that reduce foreclosure rates?
method: Exploiting discontinuous changes in servicer assignment due to bank mergers as a natural experiment, comparing foreclosure rates and loan modification offers across borrowers randomly reassigned to near versus distant servicers.
data: CFPB mortgage servicing complaint data merged with Black Knight McDash loan performance records and FDIC bank headquarters geography from 2010–2020.
key_finding: Borrowers assigned to servicers headquartered within the same metropolitan area likely receive modifications 20–35% more often and experience foreclosure 10–15% less frequently, consistent with local soft information advantages.
novelty_rationale: Servicer incentives and CMBS trustee relationships are studied, but the role of servicer geographic proximity as a mechanism for soft information in residential mortgage workouts has not been examined.
nn_title: Spatial Heterogeneity in Mortgage Terminations by Refinance, Sale and Default
assigned_label: Regional variation in mortgage market structure

research_question: Does the introduction of mandatory carbon pricing for commercial buildings, as implemented in New York City's Local Law 97, generate measurable capitalization effects that differentiate high- from low-emitting properties in transaction markets?
method: Regression discontinuity design exploiting the sharp emissions intensity thresholds that determine penalty exposure under Local Law 97, comparing transaction prices of buildings just above versus just below compliance thresholds.
data: NYC Department of Finance property transaction records, NYC Benchmarking Law building energy use intensity data, and CoStar building characteristics for all commercial properties in New York City from 2018–2025.
key_finding: Buildings subject to significant LL97 penalties are expected to trade at a 5–15% discount relative to similar buildings below the threshold, with discounts increasing as the 2030 enforcement phase tightens limits.
novelty_rationale: While green certification premiums are documented, the price effects of mandatory carbon penalty exposure creating a regulatory overhang on specific buildings via a sharp compliance cliff represents a novel identification setting.
nn_title: Commercial real estate and air pollution
assigned_label: Hedonic pricing and residential location choice

research_question: How do life-cycle wealth effects from homeownership versus renting differ across racial and ethnic groups once heterogeneity in local housing market quality and transaction cost barriers are accounted for?
method: Dynamic microsimulation model calibrated to household-level wealth trajectories from the PSID, incorporating stochastic house price appreciation, transaction costs, and rental wealth accumulation scenarios by race across 100 U.S. metropolitan areas.
data: Panel Study of Income Dynamics wealth modules from 1984–2021, Zillow ZIP-code price indices, and Urban Institute housing market opportunity indices stratified by race and geography.
key_finding: Homeownership wealth advantages are likely to be 30–50% smaller for Black households than unconditional estimates suggest once geographic sorting into lower-appreciation neighborhoods and higher transaction cost burdens are modeled.
novelty_rationale: Racial differences in homeownership returns are typically studied in cross-section; this paper uses a calibrated life-cycle simulation to integrate appreciation sorting, transaction costs, and tenure duration into a unified framework.
nn_title: The Housing Market (s) of San Diego
assigned_label: Household Housing Decisions and Wealth

research_question: Does land use deregulation that permits as-of-right multifamily development increase the risk of misallocation of new housing supply to declining-demand neighborhoods relative to rising-demand areas?
method: Quasi-experimental analysis comparing the spatial distribution of new multifamily permits before and after statewide upzoning mandates, using pre-reform demand gradients to test whether liberalization disproportionately triggers supply in low-demand locations.
data: U.S. Census building permit data at the Census tract level linked to Zillow rent-growth data for California, Oregon, and Minnesota before and after statewide zoning reforms from 2015–2023.
key_finding: Blanket upzoning without demand-sensitive incentives likely increases the share of new permits in low-demand tracts by 15–25 percentage points relative to targeted reforms, generating oversupply in areas with declining population.
novelty_rationale: Zoning liberalization is typically evaluated on aggregate supply effects; this paper examines the spatial misallocation consequence of uniform deregulation relative to targeted demand-responsive policies.
nn_title: Making housing affordable? The local effects of relaxing land-use regulation
assigned_label: Land use regulation and property values

research_question: How does the staggered adoption of e-notarization and remote online closing technology across U.S. states affect mortgage origination costs, closing timelines, and the geographic reach of lenders into underserved markets?
method: Difference-in-differences exploiting variation in state-level remote online notarization law enactment dates, comparing origination fees, time-to-close, and lender market entry in rural versus urban counties.
data: HMDA loan application records, CFPB mortgage cost data, state RON enactment dates from the National Notary Association, and FDIC rural county classifications from 2017–2023.
key_finding: RON adoption likely reduces average closing timelines by 5–10 days and origination fees by $200–$500, with disproportionate expansion of lender reach into rural counties previously constrained by physical notarization requirements.
novelty_rationale: Mortgage technology research focuses on pricing and discrimination; the supply-side geography of lender penetration as enabled by legal fintech infrastructure represents an entirely new dimension of mortgage market analysis.
nn_title: Regional Differences in Mortgage Financing Costs: A Reexamination
assigned_label: Regional variation in mortgage market structure

research_question: Does the presence of sovereign wealth fund capital in commercial real estate markets reduce market price discovery efficiency by introducing non-return-maximizing investors with political or strategic objectives?
method: Paired transaction analysis comparing SWF-involved property acquisitions against matched non-SWF transactions in the same submarket and year, using bid-ask spread dynamics around SWF market entry as an efficiency measure.
data: Preqin and Real Capital Analytics transaction records with identified SWF buyers across global gateway city office markets from 2000–2022, linked to JLL submarket vacancy and rent data.
key_finding: SWF capital entry into a submarket is likely to compress cap rates by 15–30 basis points and increase price dispersion by 10–20%, consistent with non-fundamentals-driven pricing that degrades market efficiency for subsequent arms-length transactions.
novelty_rationale: The foreign capital capitalization literature treats all international buyers similarly; this paper isolates sovereign wealth funds as a categorically distinct investor type with strategic rather than purely financial objectives.
nn_title: Correlation Dynamics and Determinants in International Securitized Real Estate M
assigned_label: Real Estate Asset Valuation and Returns

research_question: How does the spatial mismatch between the location of bus rapid transit infrastructure and the residence of transit-dependent low-income households affect neighborhood gentrification and displacement dynamics?
method: Difference-in-differences estimation comparing demographic composition and rental price trajectories in BRT-adjacent neighborhoods stratified by initial income composition, using the timing of BRT line opening as the treatment.
data: GTFS transit schedules for 15 U.S. BRT systems linked to ACS five-year demographic estimates, Zillow observed rental prices, and longitudinal tract-level income data from 2005–2022.
key_finding: BRT proximity likely generates differential gentrification depending on initial neighborhood income: low-income neighborhoods near BRT experience 8–15% faster rent growth and measurable displacement of transit-dependent households relative to wealthier treated areas.
novelty_rationale: Transit capitalization research rarely distinguishes between BRT and rail or examines equity consequences through displacement of the transit-dependent populations that infrastructure ostensibly serves.
nn_title: Gentrification Trends in New Transit‐Oriented Communities: Evidence from 14 Citi
assigned_label: Transit infrastructure capitalization in urban housing

research_question: Do natural language patterns in commercial real estate listing descriptions contain predictive information about future NOI growth and asset price appreciation beyond quantitative property characteristics?
method: NLP-based feature extraction from listing text using sentiment analysis and topic modeling, integrated into a machine learning prediction model for NOI growth benchmarked against pure-quantitative hedonic models.
data: CoStar and LoopNet commercial listing text descriptions linked to NCREIF NOI and transaction price data for office and industrial properties across 20 MSAs from 2010–2023.
key_finding: Linguistic optimism scores and specific topic clusters (e.g., management quality, renovation plans) in listing text likely explain 5–12% additional variation in 12-month NOI growth beyond quantitative predictors, providing a soft-information channel for institutional investors.
novelty_rationale: NLP has been applied to residential listings for price prediction, but its use for predicting forward operating performance in commercial real estate using listing language as a soft-information proxy is novel.
nn_title: Information value of property description: A Machine learning approach
assigned_label: Real Estate Asset Valuation and Returns

research_question: How does the mismatch between residential appraisal district boundaries and actual neighborhood housing submarket boundaries generate systematic inequities in property tax burdens across demographic groups?
method: Construction of optimal data-driven neighborhood boundaries using hedonic residual clustering, comparison of assessed-to-market-value ratios inside versus outside appraisal district boundaries, and decomposition of racial disparities in assessment error.
data: Parcel-level assessment records and arm's-length transaction prices from assessor databases in Dallas, Chicago, and Philadelphia matched to Census racial and income data from 2015–2022.
key_finding: Appraisal boundary misalignment is expected to generate assessment ratio disparities of 5–15 percentage points between demographically segregated neighborhoods, with minority-majority areas systematically over-assessed relative to majority-white submarkets.
novelty_rationale: The property tax inequity literature documents disparities but has not identified appraisal district boundary design as a structural mechanism; this paper introduces an institutional geography channel for assessment regressivity.
nn_title: The Incidence of the Land Use Regulatory Tax
assigned_label: Local tax policy and fiscal interdependence

research_question: Does exposure to sea-level rise risk heterogeneously affect REIT portfolio capital allocation, and do REITs with coastal property exposure face higher cost of capital that is not yet priced in bond markets?
method: Cross-sectional and panel analysis of REIT credit spreads, equity beta, and capital allocation decisions using FEMA and NOAA sea-level rise projections matched to REIT property-level portfolio holdings.
data: NAREIT property-level portfolio data linked to NOAA sea-level rise scenario projections, Bloomberg REIT bond issuance spread data, and SEC 10-K coastal exposure disclosures from 2010–2023.
key_finding: REITs with above-median coastal property exposure likely face 15–35 basis point higher bond spreads after 2016, but this premium understates actuarial risk by 40–60% relative to scenario-modeled expected losses, suggesting incomplete market pricing.
novelty_rationale: Climate risk in listed real estate is studied at the equity level; applying bond market pricing to identify underpricing of sea-level rise risk in REIT debt capital structure provides a new fixed-income dimension to the climate-real estate literature.
nn_title: Sea-Level Rise Exposure and Municipal Bond Yields
assigned_label: Natural disasters and real estate market dynamics

research_question: How does the staggered introduction of state-level rent stabilization policies affect the portfolio rebalancing behavior and geographic allocation decisions of institutional multifamily investors?
method: Difference-in-differences using enactment of rent stabilization laws in Oregon (2019), California (2019), and New York (2019) as staggered treatments, examining changes in institutional capital flows to multifamily markets before and after enactment.
data: Real Capital Analytics multifamily transaction records disaggregated by buyer type, Preqin institutional fund allocation data, and CoStar multifamily rent and vacancy data across treated and control MSAs from 2015–2023.
key_finding: Rent stabilization enactment likely reduces institutional multifamily acquisitions by 20–35% in regulated jurisdictions within two years and redirects capital toward adjacent unregulated markets, accelerating price appreciation spillovers.
novelty_rationale: Rent control effects on prices are well-studied, but the institutional investor portfolio reallocation response and cross-market spillover as a mechanism for policy diffusion have not been empirically examined.
nn_title: Do Wall Street Landlords Undermine Renters’ Welfare?
assigned_label: Rental Markets, Policy Effects, and Housing Supply

research_question: Does the consolidation of real estate brokerage franchises through national chain acquisitions reduce local market competition and increase commission rates relative to markets remaining served by independent brokers?
method: Difference-in-differences exploiting geographic variation in franchise acquisition timing, comparing commission rate trends and broker entry rates in acquired versus unacquired local markets using national broker identity data.
data: HMDA origination records with broker identifiers linked to NAR franchise affiliation data, state real estate commission transaction disclosures, and RealTrends brokerage merger announcements from 2010–2022.
key_finding: Franchise acquisitions reducing local HHI to a concentrated level likely increase effective commission rates by 10–20 basis points and reduce the number of independent brokers by 15–25% within three years, consistent with softened local price competition.
novelty_rationale: The brokerage literature studies commission rate determinants and agent behavior but has not examined national franchise consolidation as a competition-reducing structural change with market power implications.
nn_title: Concentration and Market Structure in Local Real Estate Markets
assigned_label: Real estate market intermediation and mechanisms

research_question: How do differences in ground lease versus fee-simple ownership of land underlying urban office buildings affect capitalization rates, leverage decisions, and lender behavior in commercial mortgage markets?
method: Matched-pair hedonic analysis comparing cap rates, LTV ratios, and default performance of ground-leased versus fee-simple office properties using transaction and loan-level data, with a theoretical model of the leasehold discount.
data: Real Capital Analytics commercial transaction data with ground lease flag, Trepp CMBS loan performance records, and ground lease term data from SEC property disclosures across major U.S. office markets from 2000–2023.
key_finding: Ground-leased office properties likely trade at a 10–20% discount to fee-simple comparables with LTV ratios 8–12 percentage points lower, reflecting lender recognition of the reversion risk embedded in long-duration leasehold structures.
novelty_rationale: Ground lease valuation has been theorized but the empirical identification of the leasehold discount in commercial mortgage pricing and lender underwriting behavior using a large matched sample remains absent from the literature.
nn_title: Gross Lease Premiums
assigned_label: Real Estate Asset Valuation and Returns

research_question: Does the geographic concentration of anchor tenant bankruptcies in retail real estate generate spatial spillovers in shopping center vacancy and cap rate compression that extend beyond the directly affected properties?
method: Difference-in-differences using sudden anchor tenant bankruptcy filings (e.g., Sears, JC Penney) as exogenous shocks, estimating price and vacancy spillovers to neighboring centers within 3-, 5-, and 10-mile rings.
data: CoStar retail property transaction and vacancy data linked to PACER bankruptcy filing dates and retailer store closure records across U.S. retail trade areas from 2010–2022.
key_finding: Anchor bankruptcy filings likely increase vacancy rates at co-located shopping centers by 3–8 percentage points and compress cap rates by 25–60 basis points within a 5-mile radius over 18 months, with effects decaying sharply beyond that distance.
novelty_rationale: Anchor tenant effects on the host property are studied, but the contagion channel through which anchor bankruptcy creates negative externalities for competing or proximate retail centers has not been systematically identified.
nn_title: Hurricane‐induced risk contagion in commercial real estate: Evidence from Hurric
assigned_label: Housing Market Efficiency and Pricing Anomalies

research_question: Can household-level debt-to-income ratios at mortgage origination predict neighborhood-level house price fragility during subsequent aggregate demand shocks, beyond aggregate LTV-based measures used in macroprudential regulation?
method: Panel regression linking ZIP-code-level distributions of DTI ratios at origination (constructed from HMDA) to house price declines during the COVID-19 shock and the Great Recession, controlling for LTV, income, and demand factors.
data: HMDA loan-level origination data with DTI ratios from 2003–2019 aggregated to the ZIP code level, linked to CoreLogic HPI and Federal Reserve Financial Accounts data on household balance sheets.
key_finding: Neighborhoods in the top quartile of average origination DTI are expected to experience 4–9 percentage points greater price declines during aggregate demand contractions relative to low-DTI neighborhoods, even after controlling for LTV and credit score distributions.
novelty_rationale: Macroprudential research emphasizes LTV as the primary collateral risk measure; this paper establishes DTI at origination as a complementary neighborhood-level vulnerability indicator with superior predictive power for price fragility in downturns.
nn_title: U.S. metropolitan house price dynamics
assigned_label: Housing market dynamics and price determinants

research_question: Does the staggered adoption of mandatory seismic retrofit ordinances for soft-story buildings causally affect residential property values, mortgage default rates, and neighborhood demographic composition in earthquake-prone cities?
method: Staggered difference-in-differences design exploiting variation in retrofit ordinance passage timing across cities and neighborhoods, combined with a hedonic regression framework to estimate treatment effects on prices, leverage, and population sorting.
data: Parcel-level transaction and mortgage records from CoreLogic matched to city-level retrofit ordinance passage dates and USGS seismic hazard maps across California, Oregon, and Washington from 2000–2023.
key_finding: Retrofit mandates likely generate a short-run price discount for unreinforced buildings followed by a capitalization premium post-completion, with displacement of lower-income renters consistent with a regulatory-induced gentrification channel.
novelty_rationale: While prior work examines generic earthquake risk capitalization, no study exploits the sharp legal discontinuity of retrofit mandates to separately identify the price, default, and demographic sorting effects of forced structural investment.
nn_title: Making housing affordable? The local effects of relaxing land-use regulation
assigned_label: Land use regulation and property values

research_question: How does the within-city spatial distribution of short-term rental activity affect the long-run depreciation of housing stock quality as measured by renovation investment and maintenance expenditures?
method: Instrumental variables estimation using the interaction of pre-Airbnb hotel density and proximity to tourist attractions as instruments for short-term rental penetration, with outcomes measured by permit issuance for renovations and hedonic quality indices.
data: Airbnb listing data from AirDNA linked to parcel-level building permit records and repeat-sales transactions across 30 U.S. cities from 2012–2023.
key_finding: High short-term rental penetration likely reduces renovation investment among non-STR adjacent owners through spillover disamenity while simultaneously increasing cosmetic upgrades among STR-operated units, creating divergent quality trajectories.
novelty_rationale: Existing STR research focuses on prices and rents but ignores the housing stock quality channel, which has implications for long-run urban decay and affordable housing supply that are entirely unexplored.
nn_title: House Prices and Rents: Microevidence from a Matched Data Set in Central London
assigned_label: Rental Markets, Policy Effects, and Housing Supply

research_question: Do mortgage lenders price the litigation risk of fair lending enforcement actions into credit supply and mortgage terms for communities that experienced prior enforcement actions?
method: Difference-in-differences estimation comparing mortgage origination volumes, denial rates, and pricing in census tracts adjacent to versus distant from lenders subject to DOJ/CFPB fair lending settlements, exploiting the quasi-random timing of enforcement actions.
data: HMDA loan-level data from 2004–2022 merged with DOJ and CFPB fair lending enforcement action records, lender identifiers, and tract-level demographic data.
key_finding: Prior enforcement actions likely generate a credit supply contraction and pricing increase in minority-majority tracts served by penalized lenders, producing the perverse outcome of reduced credit access among the intended beneficiaries of enforcement.
novelty_rationale: The literature examines discrimination in lending but not the second-order supply-side response of lenders to enforcement risk, which could explain why fair lending regulation has ambiguous welfare effects.
nn_title: Mortgage Modification and Strategic Behavior: Evidence from a Legal Settlement w
assigned_label: Mortgage Default, Delinquency, and Credit Loss

research_question: How do cross-state differences in landlord-tenant law, specifically in notice requirements and eviction moratorium authority, affect the capitalization of rental income into multifamily property values and CMBS default risk?
method: Border discontinuity regression comparing multifamily cap rates, loan spreads, and CMBS default rates in counties on opposite sides of state lines with divergent landlord-tenant legal regimes, exploiting COVID-era moratorium variation as an additional shock.
data: CoStar multifamily transaction data and CMBS loan-level performance data from TREPP merged with state-level landlord-tenant law indices and pandemic-era moratorium duration data from 2000–2023.
key_finding: Stronger tenant protections are likely capitalized into higher cap rates and wider CMBS spreads, with COVID moratorium stringency increasing default probability by 10–25 basis points for loans in high-protection states.
novelty_rationale: While foreclosure law effects on residential mortgages are well-studied, the parallel question of how tenant protection law affects commercial property valuation and commercial mortgage risk has received virtually no systematic empirical attention.
nn_title: The effects of partial-coverage rent control on the price and quantity of rental
assigned_label: Rental Markets, Policy Effects, and Housing Supply

research_question: Does the algorithmic pricing of rents by property management software, documented in recent antitrust litigation, generate measurable rent inflation beyond what local market fundamentals predict?
method: Synthetic control and event study analysis exploiting the staggered adoption of RealPage and similar algorithmic pricing platforms by large landlords, using market-level vacancy rates and income growth as controls for fundamentals.
data: Proprietary lease-level data from CoStar/RealPage market analytics merged with HMDA tract characteristics, CoreLogic rental data, and algorithmic pricing platform adoption records reconstructed from litigation discovery documents for 2010–2022.
key_finding: Algorithmic rent coordination likely inflates rents by 3–8% in concentrated markets where adopting landlords control a large share of units, with effects largest in zip codes with inelastic housing supply.
novelty_rationale: The algorithmic collusion hypothesis in rental markets is theoretically compelling but lacks rigorous causal identification; this paper would provide the first systematic empirical test using the staggered adoption design.
nn_title: Structural change in the US office market after 2019: Evidence from lease‐level
assigned_label: Rental Markets, Policy Effects, and Housing Supply

research_question: How does the spatial concentration of institutional single-family rental ownership affect the rate of neighborhood homeownership transition and long-run house price appreciation trajectories?
method: Instrumental variables approach using differential exposure to REO-to-rental program eligibility as an instrument for institutional SFR concentration, examining outcomes at the zip code and census tract level over 5–10 year horizons.
data: CoreLogic deed records identifying institutional purchasers (matched to REIT/private equity identifiers) linked to ACS homeownership rates and Zillow price indices for 2010–2023 across 50 major metros.
key_finding: High institutional SFR concentration is likely to reduce homeownership rates among lower-income households and dampen long-run price appreciation relative to fundamentals, though the effect may be heterogeneous across housing cycle phases.
novelty_rationale: Existing work on institutional SFR focuses on contemporaneous price and rent effects; the long-run neighborhood trajectory and homeownership rate effects—which are central to housing equity debates—remain unidentified.
nn_title: Do Wall Street Landlords Undermine Renters’ Welfare?
assigned_label: Housing market dynamics and price determinants

research_question: Can real-time satellite-derived nighttime light intensity and foot traffic data improve the nowcasting and forecasting of commercial real estate net operating income relative to traditional lagged appraisal-based measures?
method: Machine learning regression models (gradient boosting, neural networks) combining high-frequency satellite nighttime light, cell phone mobility, and OpenStreetMap foot traffic data to predict NCREIF NOI at the property level, benchmarked against lagged appraisal returns.
data: NCREIF property-level NOI data from 2010–2023 matched to NASA VIIRS nighttime lights, SafeGraph/Veraset foot traffic, and Google Places review velocity for approximately 5,000 commercial properties across major U.S. metros.
key_finding: High-frequency satellite and mobility data likely reduce NOI forecast errors by 15–30% at the quarterly horizon, with gains concentrated in retail and hospitality sectors where physical activity directly maps to revenue.
novelty_rationale: Existing real estate forecasting uses lagged financial fundamentals; this paper introduces truly high-frequency alternative data for property-level nowcasting, bridging remote sensing and real estate finance literatures.
nn_title: Combining machine learning and econometrics: Application to commercial real esta
assigned_label: Hedonic pricing and residential location choice

research_question: How does the demutualization and privatization of mutual savings banks affect mortgage lending standards, approval rates, and subsequent loan performance in the communities they historically served?
method: Difference-in-differences analysis exploiting the staggered conversion of mutual savings institutions to stock form, comparing pre- and post-conversion mortgage origination and default outcomes in counties where converted versus unconverted institutions operate.
data: FDIC call report data on mutual-to-stock conversions from 1980–2020 merged with HMDA mortgage records and CoreLogic loan performance data for the counties and borrower segments most dependent on each institution.
key_finding: Demutualization likely causes a shift toward higher-margin but riskier mortgage products in affiliated markets, increasing short-run origination volume but raising subsequent default rates, particularly for lower-income borrowers.
novelty_rationale: Savings institution ownership structure has been largely ignored in post-crisis mortgage research despite its historical importance in community lending; demutualization events provide clean identification of ownership form effects.
nn_title: Originator Performance, CMBS Structures, and the Risk of Commercial Mortgages
assigned_label: Securitization, Risk Retention, and Intermediation

research_question: Does the presence and density of urban tree canopy have heterogeneous capitalization effects across income levels and racial groups, and do these effects change following major storm events that selectively damage canopy in low-income areas?
method: Difference-in-differences combined with spatial regression discontinuity design exploiting storm-induced canopy loss variation, with heterogeneous effects estimated across demographic subgroups using interacted hedonic models.
data: USDA Urban Tree Canopy Assessment data linked to parcel-level transactions from county assessor records, pre- and post-storm satellite canopy measurements, and ACS demographic data for 20 cities over 2005–2022.
key_finding: Tree canopy capitalization is likely regressive—larger in high-income areas—and storm-induced canopy loss in low-income neighborhoods is unlikely to be replaced through public or private investment, widening environmental amenity gaps.
novelty_rationale: Prior green amenity hedonic studies treat canopy as static; this paper exploits exogenous canopy destruction to separately identify environmental justice dimensions of tree canopy valuation that static studies cannot address.
nn_title: What Is a Tree Worth? Green‐City Strategies, Signaling and Housing Prices
assigned_label: Hedonic pricing and residential location choice

research_question: How do REIT managerial incentives, specifically the structure of performance-based compensation tied to total shareholder return versus funds from operations, affect the quality and location of property acquisitions and subsequent operating performance?
method: Panel regression analysis exploiting time-series variation in compensation contract structure within REITs, using CEO contract disclosure from proxy statements, with acquisition quality measured by subsequent NOI growth and cap rate spreads.
data: Execucomp CEO compensation data linked to SNL Real Estate REIT acquisition records, property-level NOI data, and proxy statement compensation structure disclosures for all publicly traded equity REITs from 1993–2022.
key_finding: REITs with TSR-based compensation are likely to favor growth-oriented acquisitions in overheated markets, generating negative abnormal operating returns, while FFO-based compensation aligns managers toward income-producing asset quality.
novelty_rationale: REIT compensation research focuses on aggregate measures; connecting specific compensation metric design—TSR versus FFO benchmarks—to the ex-post quality and location of individual property acquisitions is unexplored.
nn_title: The Effect of Corporate Governance on Investment: Evidence from Real Estate Inve
assigned_label: Real Estate Asset Valuation and Returns

research_question: Does the exit of community development financial institutions (CDFIs) from local mortgage markets, following funding withdrawals or organizational failure, generate measurable credit gaps and house price declines in underserved neighborhoods?
method: Event study combined with difference-in-differences analysis exploiting CDFI organizational closures and New Markets Tax Credit award terminations as shocks to targeted mortgage credit supply in low-income census tracts.
data: CDFI Fund award and certification data matched to HMDA originations, CoreLogic property transactions, and ACS tract-level demographic data for 2000–2022 in census tracts historically served by CDFIs.
key_finding: CDFI exit likely causes a 10–20% decline in mortgage originations to low-income and minority borrowers in affected tracts and a modest house price decline, with larger effects in areas where the CDFI had higher prior market share.
novelty_rationale: CDFIs are rarely studied as distinct mortgage market participants despite their explicit mission to serve underserved communities; their exit provides a clean supply shock unavailable in studies of mainstream lenders.
nn_title: Did Affordable Housing Legislation Contribute to the Subprime Securities Boom?
assigned_label: Regional variation in mortgage market structure

research_question: How does the staggered adoption of crime-free housing ordinances—which allow landlords to evict tenants based on arrests rather than convictions—affect rental prices, vacancy rates, and racial segregation in affected neighborhoods?
method: Staggered difference-in-differences exploiting variation in crime-free housing ordinance adoption timing across municipalities, measuring effects on rental price indices, HMDA mortgage applications, and ACS segregation measures.
data: University of Illinois Crime-Free Housing Ordinance database matched to Zillow rental indices, HMDA data, and ACS demographic and vacancy data for 500+ municipalities from 2000–2022.
key_finding: Crime-free ordinances likely reduce rental prices in affected neighborhoods due to increased vacancy from selective evictions and deter Black and Hispanic renters disproportionately, increasing residential segregation.
novelty_rationale: Crime-free housing ordinances are widespread yet empirically understudied in the real estate economics literature; their rental market and segregation effects raise fundamental questions about housing discrimination law.
nn_title: Making housing affordable? The local effects of relaxing land-use regulation
assigned_label: Racial discrimination in housing markets

research_question: Does the retirement of coal-fired power plants, driven by EPA regulatory action, generate heterogeneous house price effects across nearby residential and industrial neighborhoods as air quality improves and economic activity declines?
method: Staggered difference-in-differences exploiting the timing of EPA-regulated coal plant retirements across census tracts at varying distances, separately estimating air quality improvement and local employment loss channels.
data: EPA National Emissions Inventory and plant retirement records matched to CoreLogic residential transaction prices, EPA air quality monitoring station data, BLS county-level employment data, and HMDA originations from 2005–2022.
key_finding: Residential price effects of plant retirement are likely positive near plant sites due to air quality improvements but negative in economically dependent communities further away, with a net effect dependent on the industrial versus residential composition of nearby land use.
novelty_rationale: Transition risk in real estate is studied primarily for coastal and flood risk; this paper identifies an underexplored fossil fuel transition channel with a clean causal design based on regulatory plant retirement timing.
nn_title: Evidence of the Dual Nature of Property Value Recovery Following Environmental R
assigned_label: Environmental hazards and property valuation

research_question: How does the spatial externality from neighboring property tax delinquency affect individual homeowner mortgage default and maintenance investment decisions, beyond the direct house price effects of foreclosure spillovers?
method: Instrumental variables estimation using local government fiscal stress and state aid formula cutoffs as instruments for area-level tax delinquency rates, with individual mortgage performance and permit records as outcomes.
data: County assessor tax delinquency records linked to CoreLogic mortgage performance data and building permit databases for 30 cities from 2000–2020, with state aid formula parameters obtained from state budget documents.
key_finding: Neighborhood tax delinquency concentration likely increases individual mortgage default hazards by 5–15% and reduces renovation investment independent of house price depreciation, representing a fiscal-to-credit contagion channel.
novelty_rationale: Tax delinquency is a distinct channel from foreclosure externalities studied in prior work, connecting public finance and mortgage default literatures through a mechanism involving neighborhood fiscal viability rather than property condition.
nn_title: Foreclosure Externalities: Have We Confused the Cure with the Disease?
assigned_label: Housing Market Efficiency and Pricing Anomalies

research_question: Does the introduction of mandatory climate risk disclosure requirements for commercial real estate transactions, as implemented in select European jurisdictions, cause investors to reprice physical climate risk in CMBS markets?
method: Difference-in-differences analysis comparing CMBS loan spreads and LTV ratios before and after mandatory disclosure implementation in treating jurisdictions versus matched non-implementing jurisdictions using European ABS data.
data: European Central Bank ESRB commercial real estate loan data, CMBS loan tape data from Intex/Trepp Europe, physical climate risk scores from JRC/ClimateAi, and jurisdiction-level disclosure regulation enactment dates from 2010–2023.
key_finding: Mandatory climate disclosure likely causes a 15–40 basis point increase in loan spreads for properties in high physical risk zones post-implementation, with larger repricing for flood and heat stress risk than for transition risk.
novelty_rationale: Existing climate-real estate research examines market reactions to salient natural disasters; mandatory disclosure regimes provide an institutional mechanism with a clean on/off policy timing that allows identification of information-driven repricing.
nn_title: When climate meets real estate: A survey of the literature
assigned_label: Natural disasters and real estate market dynamics

research_question: How does the interaction between mortgage forbearance take-up rates during the COVID-19 pandemic and local housing supply constraints determine the extent of house price appreciation versus inventory shortfalls in post-pandemic housing markets?
method: Two-stage instrumental variables where forbearance take-up is instrumented by pre-pandemic industry employment composition (hospitality/retail share) and supply constraints are measured by Wharton RZSI, estimating the interaction effect on price appreciation from 2020–2023.
data: Black Knight mortgage performance data on forbearance take-up at the zip code level linked to Zillow house price indices, housing inventory measures, BLS industry employment data, and the Wharton Residential Land Use Regulatory Index for 2018–2023.
key_finding: Forbearance-induced inventory suppression in supply-constrained markets likely accounts for 20–40% of the post-pandemic house price surge beyond what demand fundamentals predict, with inventory effects persisting 18–24 months post-forbearance.
novelty_rationale: While pandemic forbearance and supply constraints are individually studied, their interaction as a joint mechanism generating the extraordinary 2020–2022 price surge—through inventory withdrawal rather than direct demand—has not been formally identified.
nn_title: Housing markets in a pandemic: Evidence from historical outbreaks
assigned_label: Pandemics, Disease, and Urban Property Markets

research_question: Do real estate investment trusts with higher employee satisfaction scores, as revealed by Glassdoor employer ratings, exhibit better operating efficiency, lower tenant turnover, and superior long-run stock market performance?
method: Panel regression analysis linking firm-level Glassdoor ratings to REIT operating performance metrics (NOI growth, FFO per share, same-store occupancy) and risk-adjusted stock returns, controlling for property type, size, and market fixed effects.
data: Glassdoor employer review data for publicly traded equity REITs matched to SNL/CoStar property-level operating data and CRSP stock returns for all U.S. equity REITs from 2008–2022.
key_finding: Higher employee satisfaction is likely positively associated with tenant retention and same-store NOI growth, and predicts positive abnormal stock returns over 12–24 month horizons, consistent with soft human capital being a priced asset in real estate operations.
novelty_rationale: REIT performance research focuses on financial structure and governance but ignores operational human capital; Glassdoor data enables the first systematic test of whether employee satisfaction is a value-relevant soft intangible in real estate.
nn_title: Employee productivity and REIT performance
assigned_label: Real Estate Asset Valuation and Returns

research_question: How do mortgage servicer incentive structures, specifically the differential between performing and delinquent loan servicing fees, affect the speed and depth of loss mitigation responses to neighborhood-level house price shocks?
method: Quasi-experimental analysis using the 2014 CFPB servicer compensation rule change as a shock to servicer incentive structures, comparing loan modification rates and loss severity across servicers with high versus low pre-reform delinquent servicing fee revenue.
data: Black Knight McDash loan-level servicing data linked to CFPB supervisory examination records, servicer identity, and CoreLogic collateral values for all GSE and private label mortgages from 2010–2020.
key_finding: Servicers with higher delinquent loan fee revenue before the rule change likely reduced modification rates and extended delinquency timelines, increasing loss severity by 8–15 percentage points relative to servicers already aligned with borrower-friendly modification practices.
novelty_rationale: Servicer incentive effects on loss mitigation behavior are theoretically well-understood but empirically hard to identify; the 2014 CFPB rule provides a clean cross-servicer shock to the compensation structure that prior studies have not exploited.
nn_title: Price Distortions Induced by the Revenue Structure of Federally-Sponsored Mortga
assigned_label: Mortgage Default, Delinquency, and Credit Loss

research_question: Can the co-movement structure of commercial real estate cap rates across property types and geographies serve as an early warning indicator of financial system vulnerability to real estate cycle turning points?
method: Construction of a time-varying cap rate correlation network using Dynamic Conditional Correlation (DCC) models across 20 property-type/metro combinations, testing whether network density predicts subsequent CMBS delinquency surges and bank stress test failures.
data: CoStar transaction-based cap rate series by property type and metropolitan area from 1995–2023, linked to CMBS delinquency rates from Trepp and bank stress test results from Federal Reserve DFAST disclosures.
key_finding: Elevated cap rate correlation network density across property types and geographies likely predicts CMBS delinquency increases 2–4 quarters ahead, providing a systemic risk indicator superior to individual cap rate levels or spreads.
novelty_rationale: Systemic risk measurement in real estate uses price-based connectedness measures; using cap rate co-movement networks links fundamental valuation synchronization to downstream credit risk in a novel early warning framework.
nn_title: On the Price Comovement of U.S. Residential Real Estate Markets
assigned_label: Real Estate Asset Valuation and Returns

research_question: How does the match quality between a real estate agent's ethnic background and the neighborhood's dominant ethnic composition affect listing price strategy, time on market, and final transaction prices across buyer and seller roles?
method: Agent-client ethnic match analysis using name-based ethnicity imputation for agents and sellers/buyers in HMDA and county deed records, estimating same-ethnicity match effects within neighborhood-year cells controlling for property characteristics.
data: County deed records for 15 diverse U.S. metros from 2005–2022 merged with state real estate license holder data, HMDA borrower records, and census tract ethnic composition from the ACS.
key_finding: Ethnic match between agent and neighborhood likely improves listing prices and reduces time on market for sellers through information advantages and network effects, with larger effects in neighborhoods with high ethnic concentration and lower market liquidity.
novelty_rationale: Prior work on agent ethnicity examines discrimination; this paper instead examines ethnicity as a positive information and network endowment that creates differential performance, distinct from the homophily evidence in the corpus.
nn_title: Ethnicity in housing markets: Buyers, sellers and agents
assigned_label: Racial discrimination in housing markets

research_question: Does the density of distressed commercial properties—vacant retail centers and zombie office buildings—generate measurable spillover effects on adjacent residential house prices and neighborhood economic vitality indicators?
method: Spatial difference-in-differences exploiting the differential timing of anchor tenant departures and mass office vacancy events, estimating residential price effects within 0.25–1 mile rings over 3–7 year post-event windows.
data: CoStar commercial occupancy and vacancy records linked to CoreLogic residential parcel transactions, business license records, and Opportunity Insights local economic activity measures for 50 U.S. metros from 2005–2023.
key_finding: Commercial blight likely generates negative residential price spillovers of 2–6% within a quarter mile, with effects persisting and expanding spatially over time and being larger in suburban strip mall contexts than in dense urban environments.
novelty_rationale: The literature examines commercial-to-residential conversion potential but not the ongoing negative externality of zombie commercial vacancy on nearby residential values, which is empirically distinct from foreclosure or vacant residential property spillovers.
nn_title: The contagion effect of foreclosed properties
assigned_label: Housing Market Efficiency and Pricing Anomalies

research_question: How does the geographic dispersion of a publicly traded real estate company's property portfolio across time zones affect the efficiency of information processing in stock prices, analyst forecast accuracy, and institutional investor engagement?
method: Panel regression analysis using time zone dispersion of property holdings as a proxy for monitoring costs, testing its relationship with analyst coverage errors, price non-synchronicity measures, and institutional investor portfolio concentration.
data: CoStar REIT property location data linked to CRSP/Compustat financial data, IBES analyst forecast records, and Thomson Reuters 13F institutional holdings for all U.S. equity REITs from 1994–2022.
key_finding: REITs with high geographic time zone dispersion likely exhibit larger analyst forecast errors and greater price non-synchronicity, reflecting higher information processing costs, with institutional investors discounting time-zone-dispersed portfolios in their ownership decisions.
novelty_rationale: Geographic diversification in REITs is studied for operating performance but not for information processing costs; time zone dispersion provides a novel, exogenous-like measure of monitoring difficulty distinct from standard concentration metrics.
nn_title: Asset productivity, local information diffusion, and commercial real estate retu
assigned_label: Real Estate Asset Valuation and Returns

research_question: Does the conversion of large urban office campuses to mixed-use development—including residential and retail components—generate positive fiscal externalities for municipal tax revenue beyond the direct tax base increase from new construction?
method: Synthetic control estimation comparing municipal property tax revenue trajectories, sales tax receipts, and permit activity between cities completing major campus conversion projects and carefully constructed synthetic control municipalities from 2005–2023.
data: Local government financial data from the Census of Governments merged with CoStar redevelopment project completion records, county assessor property tax records, and state sales tax data for 40 major campus conversion projects.
key_finding: Campus conversions likely generate fiscal multiplier effects of 1.3–2.0 through increased nearby property values and retail activity, but these gains may take 5–10 years to materialize and are highly dependent on transit access and local housing market conditions.
novelty_rationale: Urban redevelopment fiscal impact studies rely on developer-commissioned pro formas; synthetic control estimation of actual fiscal trajectories would provide the first rigorous causal evidence on mixed-use campus conversion externalities.
nn_title: Municipal Improvement and Finance as Effected by the Untaxing of Improvements an
assigned_label: Local tax policy and fiscal interdependence

research_question: How does heterogeneity in state homestead exemption laws—which shield a portion of home equity from creditor claims in bankruptcy—affect the composition of households' non-housing portfolio, specifically their entrepreneurial risk-taking and equity market participation?
method: Regression discontinuity design exploiting homestead exemption value thresholds within states combined with cross-state variation in exemption generosity, using PSID and SCF data on portfolio composition, self-employment income, and business ownership.
data: Panel Study of Income Dynamics and Survey of Consumer Finances household-level wealth and portfolio data merged with state homestead exemption schedules from Bankr-Info and state statutes for 1984–2022.
key_finding: More generous homestead exemptions likely increase equity market participation and entrepreneurial activity among homeowners near the exemption threshold, consistent with home equity serving as collateral that enables risk-taking when protected from forced sale.
novelty_rationale: Homestead exemptions are studied for mortgage credit rationing but the portfolio theory channel—whereby home equity protection shifts household risk-taking in non-housing investments—represents a novel intersection of housing policy and household finance.
nn_title: Bankruptcy Exemption, Home Equity and Mortgage Credit
assigned_label: Household tenure choice and homeownership decisions

research_question: Does the staggered adoption of real-time property data sharing between municipal assessors and mortgage lenders through automated valuation model integrations reduce appraisal bias and improve mortgage pricing efficiency in minority communities?
method: Difference-in-differences analysis exploiting variation in the timing of county-level assessor-AVM data integration agreements, measuring effects on appraisal-to-contract price ratios, minority borrower denial rates, and mortgage pricing disparities.
data: HMDA loan-level application data linked to county assessor digital record-sharing program implementation dates, CoreLogic appraisal values and contract prices, and census tract racial composition for 2010–2022.
key_finding: AVM-assessor integration likely reduces appraisal shortfall rates for minority borrowers by 15–25% and narrows mortgage rate disparities by improving price discovery, but may also reduce appraiser discretion in ways that disadvantage atypical properties in diverse neighborhoods.
novelty_rationale: Appraisal reform debates focus on appraiser demographics and bias but ignore the technological infrastructure through which information flows to appraisers; AVM integration represents a governance intervention with directly testable consequences for racial equity in appraisal.
